records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imbittered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Imbittering.]

defs: To make bitter; hence, to make distressing or more distressing; to make sad, morose, sour, or malignant.
quotes: Is there anything that more imbitters the enjoyment of this life than shame?
Imbittered against each other by former contests.
